title: Data Improvement Model Based on ECG Biometric for User Authentication and Identification

data.dataset_name: PhysioNet Computing in Cardiology 2018 (You Snooze You Win)

method.input_representation: Handcrafted features (P, Q, R, S, T wave amplitudes and durations)
method.training_strategy: 80% training, 20% testing, grid search for hyperparameter tuning, 3-subset cross-validation

evaluation.context: Achieved using the DETECT model on the PhysioNet 2018 dataset with 1985 subjects
Achieved using the DETECT model on the PhysioNet 2018 dataset with 1985 subjects

evaluation_protocol: One-vs-rest classification using Random Forest base estimators, 80/20 split, 3-fold cross-validation for tuning.

reported_limitations: Performance reduces as the number of target classes (subjects) increases
Need for periodic training set updates due to natural anatomical variability over time

key_contribution: Proposed DETECT data improvement model incorporating data augmentation and IQR-based outlier removal
Demonstrated ECG biometric scalability using a large-scale dataset of ~2000 subjects
main_strength: Significantly larger subject population than most existing ECG biometric studies
Systematic evaluation of data improvement steps (augmentation, outlier removal)
main_weakness: Used lab-collected sleep data rather than real-world wearable data
No testing against active spoofing or presentation attacks
insights.notes_for_review: The paper emphasizes that data quality and quantity are more critical than the specific ML algorithm for large-scale biometric identification.
